text.title: Testing in JavaScript / TypeScript Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of testing-javascript enforces. Each rule has a statement, a rationale anchored to industry practice, and a source citation.

rule.statement: Choose Vitest when the project uses Vite, ESM-first, or Vue/Svelte. Choose Jest only for legacy CRA, React Native, or webpack-only stacks.
rule.rationale: Mismatched runner + bundler is the #1 source of `Cannot use import statement outside a module` errors. Match the runner to the build tool.

rule.statement: Query React components with `getByRole`, `getByLabelText`, `getByText`. Never use `getByTestId` unless none of the others fit.
rule.rationale: ARIA-first queries align tests with how assistive tech sees the UI; `data-testid` is a fallback that ties tests to implementation.

rule.statement: Mock outbound HTTP at the network layer with MSW handlers, not by stubbing `fetch` or `axios` modules.
rule.rationale: MSW catches typos in URL or method that module-level mocks paper over; same handlers work in tests + Storybook + dev.

rule.statement: Always pair `vi.useFakeTimers()` / `jest.useFakeTimers()` with a teardown that calls `useRealTimers()`.
rule.rationale: Leaked fake timers poison subsequent tests; the next file that uses `setTimeout` silently never resolves.

rule.statement: Set `testEnvironment` explicitly: `jsdom` for component tests, `node` for plain logic.
rule.rationale: jsdom in pure-logic tests adds 200ms per file with no benefit; node env in component tests crashes on `window`.

rule.statement: Use `await screen.findByRole(...)` for async UI. Never wrap `getByRole` in `setTimeout`/`waitFor` manually unless required.
rule.rationale: `findByRole` retries internally with the right defaults; manual polling drifts out of sync with React 18 concurrent rendering.
